title: Capture matrix (Playwright projects-matrix) + baseline-hash visual regression receipt
goal: Per spec §6.4.4 (AC-VISUAL-DEBUGGER-MATRIX) implement the capture matrix: Playwright projects-matrix shape, each scenario produces a manifest entry with scenario_id/route/viewport/color_scheme/locale/edge_state_tag/wait_for/mask_selectors/baseline_hash; baselines content-addressed under .GOV/visual_baselines/; drift triggers a typed visual-regression receipt.
expected_diff_shape: Updated playwright.config.ts with projects matrix; new tests/visual/capture_matrix.ts manifest schema + utilities; new directory .GOV/visual_baselines/ with .gitignore stub explaining content-addressed storage; HBR_VIS_REGRESSION typed receipt schema added to shared schemas dir.
unblock_conditions: MT-021 Playwright + driver; MT-022 console scan (every capture pairs with a scan); MT-006 HBR_VIOLATION receipt schema (visual regression piggybacks on the same canonical-JSON receipt pattern).
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.4.4. Manifest schema: `interface CaptureMatrixEntry { scenario_id: string; route: string; viewport: { width: number; height: number }; color_scheme: 'light'|'dark'; locale: string; edge_state_tag: 'normal'|'empty'|'error'|'loading'|'multi_item'; wait_for: string; mask_selectors: string[]; baseline_hash: string; }`. playwright.config.ts projects array enumerates the matrix: at minimum the trio required by HBR-VIS-003 (one normal viewport, one constrained viewport, one edge state). Baselines content-addressed: filename is sha256(rendered-png), stored under .GOV/visual_baselines/<sha>.png; the manifest carries baseline_hash. On capture: compute hash, compare against manifest's baseline_hash via pixel-diff threshold; on drift emit HBR_VIS_REGRESSION receipt (canonical-JSON per MT-006 pattern): `{ receipt_kind: "HBR_VIS_REGRESSION", schema_version: 1, receipt_uuid: Uuid v7, scenario_id, baseline_hash, captured_hash, pixel_diff_percent, drift_regions: [{x,y,w,h}] }`. .GOV/visual_baselines/.gitignore: explain that baseline PNGs MAY be checked in or stored externally depending on disk-space policy (per [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-008] this is configurable; default = check in; cap at 100MB total directory size; if exceeded, externalize via configured external-baselines path). hbr-vis-regression.schema.json: JSON schema validated by both the Playwright test and a Node validator (mirrors MT-006 pattern). capture_matrix.spec.ts: walks the matrix, calls page.screenshot per entry with mask_selectors honored (via Playwright's clip + page.locator masking), pairs with MT-022 console scan, on drift emits the receipt. Tests: fixture matrix with two scenarios, one passing one with deliberate drift; assert receipt produced on drift; assert no receipt on identical render.
proof_commands: cd app && pnpm exec playwright test tests/visual/capture_matrix.spec.ts
risk_if_missed: Without capture matrix, HBR-VIS-003 has no evidence; visual regressions across viewports/states go undetected.
validator_focus: Baselines are content-addressed (filename == sha256); drift emits the canonical-JSON receipt validated against the schema; mask_selectors actually mask (verify by rendering an animated region and confirming no false drift).

minimum_controls: Content-addressed baseline storage prevents accidental overwrite of an old baseline (filename == hash).
Baseline directory size cap is enforced (test asserts dir < 100MB or external storage configured).
Mask selectors are validated by a fixture with animated content to prove they actually mask.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: playwright.config.ts defines a three-project matrix per HBR-VIS-003 (normal 1280x720 light en-US, constrained 390x844 dark en-US, edge-empty 1024x768 light nl-NL); capture_matrix.ts implements content-addressed baselines (filename = sha256(captured png)), bootstrap-hash mechanic, HBR_VIS_REGRESSION receipt with canonical JSON sorted keys + UUID v7, mask_selectors honored via Playwright locator masks, baseline directory size cap (100MB), and Ajv schema validation against .GOV/roles_shared/schemas/hbr-vis-regression.schema.json.
